title: The Spider and the Ghost of the Fly
content:
Once I loved a spider
	     When I was born a fly,
	     A velvet-footed spider
	     With a gown of rainbow-dye.
	     She ate my wings and gloated.
	     She bound me with a hair.
	     She drove me to her parlor
	     Above her winding stair.
	     To educate young spiders
	     She took me all apart.
	     My ghost came back to haunt her.
	     I saw her eat my heart.
meaning:
"The Spider and the Ghost of the Fly" is a quintessential example of Vachel Lindsay's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
